pick.rationale: Gonzaga holds a commanding statistical advantage with an 11th-ranked national profile and elite +28.16 adjusted efficiency margin, featuring the 9th-ranked defense nationally. Kennesaw State's SRS of 0.13 ranks 151st with a weak schedule and mediocre 21-13 record against Conference USA competition, revealing a gap of approximately 140 ranking spots between these teams. While Gonzaga has been without star forward Braden Huff since mid-January due to a knee injury, the Bulldogs have demonstrated overwhelming dominance without him, posting a 30-3 record and WCC championship. The 3/14 seed line historically produces victories for the favorite approximately 85% of the time, and Gonzaga's elite two-way metrics provide minimal vulnerability for Kennesaw State to exploit despite their Conference USA tournament momentum.

reasoningStep.title: Gonzaga vs Kennesaw State - 3/14 Seed Mismatch Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Analyzed comprehensive statistical profiles for both teams, revealing a massive disparity favoring Gonzaga. The Bulldogs rank 11th nationally with elite offensive and defensive efficiency, while Kennesaw State ranks 151st in SRS with a weak schedule and 6th-place conference finish. Despite Gonzaga's injury to Braden Huff, their 30-3 record without him demonstrates sufficient depth to dominate this matchup. The 140+ spot ranking gap and historical 3/14 seed patterns support a high-confidence pick for Gonzaga.
evidence: Gonzaga ranked 11th in both KenPom and Torvik with 30-3 record and +28.16 adj. efficiency margin
Gonzaga's defense ranked 9th nationally (93.97 adj. DE)
Kennesaw State SRS of 0.13 ranks 151st nationally with -2.00 SOS (184th), indicating weak competition
Kennesaw State finished 21-13 overall, 10-10 (6th place) in Conference USA before tournament title run
Braden Huff injury status: Out since mid-January with knee injury; Few 'less than optimistic' for first weekend
Historical 3/14 matchup data: 3-seeds win ~85% of games at this seed line
